summary: Chromium credential and cookie stealer for Windows
details: @ibrahim1337/baksen@2.0.6 is a purpose-built Windows infostealer published as an npm package. It enumerates Chrome, Brave, and Edge user-data directories under %LOCALAPPDATA%, copies the Cookies and 'Login Data' SQLite databases, DPAPI-decrypts the browser master key, and loads a shipped native addon (build/Release/debugelevator.node, ~680KB) that attaches as a debugger to the running browser to capture the v20 App-Bound AES key. getPasswords() runs `SELECT origin_url, username_value, password_value FROM logins` against the copied database and writes decrypted URL/username/password records and Netscape-format cookies to local files. The main entry (index.jsc) and license module (src/license.jsc) are shipped as bytenode-compiled V8 bytecode and obfuscated with js-confuser, concealing the full code path around the native decryption addon. A license module fetches a mutable key list from https://raw.githubusercontent.com/tabo1337/SaatPCBKod/refs/heads/main/key.txt (unpinned third-party GitHub user, no commit hash), computes a license hash, and passes it into the native addon, giving that account live remote control over gating and addon behavior. Installing this package places a working browser credential and cookie extractor into the developer's environment; running it on Windows yields plaintext saved passwords and live session cookies for any installed Chromium browser profile.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
